target:
data/wins.json WIN-044 detail_evidence — single-character editorial fix removing a fragmentary 'stency.' suffix that appears to be leftover scaffolding from a prior 'consistency' edit. The current text reads '…not because it was tested against nature.stency.

'; the fragment 'stency.' has no referent (the word 'consistency' already opened the same sentence) and reads as a botched edit. Replace with 'nature.

' so the sentence ends cleanly. No substance change.
patch_instructions:
Single substring edit on data/wins.json WIN-044 (id='044'). Replace the unique 9-character anchor 'nature.stency.

' with 'nature.

' inside detail_evidence. Anchor verified to occur exactly once in the entire file and exactly once in WIN-044.detail_evidence (count=1, count=1). After patch, the sentence ends cleanly: '…not because it was tested against nature.' followed by a paragraph break to the 'Direct dome source:' technical block.

patches.rationale: The fragment 'stency.' is leftover edit scaffolding. The word 'consistency' is already used as the lead noun two clauses earlier ('…internal algebraic consistency —'); the trailing fragment is not a sentence component, has no antecedent, and is presumably a partial-replace artefact from when an earlier draft restructured this sentence. Removing it is a zero-substance editorial cleanup that ends the eclipse-test paragraph cleanly. The Self-Contradicted verdict, the version-instability lead, the Section 1.8 cross-reference, and the technical reproduction paragraph are all unchanged.

re_examination_findings.summary: Mode 2b Phase 3 substantive re-examination of attention item ATT-2026-04-15T19:42:38.726Z-3. The decider's patch (lead-with-version-instability + Section 1.8 cross-ref) succeeded — the current detail_evidence opens with the FSF version-instability claim (HAD 1.47 vs 1.89, BOU 5,715 vs 9,983 km) and includes the Section 1.8 anchor. detail_verdict_text restates the same self-contradiction thesis and cross-links Section 1.8. The Self-Contradicted verdict is structurally supported cleanly. One editorial defect surfaced during the re-read: a fragmentary 'stency.' suffix in P2. This expansion patches that fragment; no substantive analyst rewrite is warranted.
re_examination_findings.structural_argument_lead: The opening sentence states the version-instability finding directly: HAD 1.47 (V51) vs 1.89 (V50.6), 28% discrepancy, BOU radius shift 5,715 → 9,983 km. This is the strongest plain-language form of the Self-Contradicted argument and is in the lead position.
other_defects_noted:
Editorial: 'nature.stency.

' fragment in P2 — patched here.

Structural quibble (NOT patched, NOT a defect): P2 ends 'this WIN confirms nothing beyond internal algebraic consistency …' and P3 opens 'Direct dome source: check_eclipse_fsf.py defines FSF as …'. P3's technical reproduction details (V51 ellipse H(r) reproduces ~3%, V50.6 exponential H(r) does not reproduce HAD) overlap conceptually with P1's version-instability lead. A future restructuring pass might fold P3's technical specificity into P1 or trim the redundancy. Not in scope for this Phase 3 — the verdict is supported as written, and the redundancy reinforces rather than weakens the argument.

kernel_of_truth: There is no kernel-of-truth applicable here — this is an editorial cleanup, not a substantive claim. (The kernel-of-truth treatment for the dome's underlying FSF claim already lives in WIN-044's body: the formula does in fact reproduce E001–E009 published values to within ~3% under V51 parameters, which is genuine algebraic consistency. WIN-044 acknowledges that and shows it is not the same as empirical confirmation.)

alternative_considered_rejected.alternative: Leave the 'stency.' fragment in place — it is a tiny defect that does not affect verdict support.
alternative_considered_rejected.reason_rejected: True that it does not affect the verdict, but it is a visible textual defect in published prose that any reader will notice and reads as carelessness. A site that critiques the dome model for sloppy execution should not itself ship typos in the WINs that articulate the critique. The fix is one substring replacement with a verified-unique anchor; the cost is trivial and the credibility benefit is real.

alternative_considered_rejected.alternative: Restructure WIN-044 detail_evidence to fold P3 (technical reproduction details) into P1 (version-instability lead) and remove conceptual overlap.
alternative_considered_rejected.reason_rejected: Out of scope for a Mode 2b Phase 3 substantive re-examination of a clean-up-attention-inbox item. The attention-item's stated question — 'verify restructured text still supports Self-Contradicted verdict cleanly' — is answered (yes). Restructuring beyond that exceeds the singleton-substantive remit and risks regressing the Section 1.8 cross-reference work and the version-instability lead that the decider's prior patch installed. If a curmudgeon flags the P1↔P3 overlap as a defect-grade finding in a future cycle, that is the appropriate trigger for restructuring.

alternative_considered_rejected.alternative: Write an issue-proposal pointing the decider at the typo rather than an expansion with a patch.
alternative_considered_rejected.reason_rejected: An expansion with a verified-unique substring patch is the correct unit for a 1-anchor 1-replacement editorial fix. Issue-proposals are the signaling unit for assigned-issue resolution (ISS-NNNN). There is no ISS for this typo, the patch is small enough to integrate directly, and a self-contained expansion is the lowest-friction path. The decider applies, build/test, done.

anticipated_objections.objection: Why is this not a curmudgeon-c-N hole? Editorial defects are usually caught in adversarial review.
anticipated_objections.response: Mode 2b Phase 3 re-examination is an analyst pass with fresh eyes on text the decider patched some time ago. Surfacing a small editorial defect during that re-read is exactly the use-case for the substantive-singleton path: original analysis still holds (verdict supported cleanly), but the re-read caught one defect worth fixing. The curmudgeon may have already noted this and not yet had a cycle-N pass on WIN-044 since the last decider patch; even if so, fixing it now removes a finding from the next cycle's punch list.

anticipated_objections.objection: Are you sure 'stency.' is not part of intended text — for example, a stylistic choice, a footnote marker, a reference to some specific construct?
anticipated_objections.response:
Yes. 'stency' is not a word in English. It contains no or … wrapper that would indicate it's a citation/footnote/code-symbol, and the surrounding markup is plain '

'. The only string in WIN-044 that ends in 'stency' as a complete word is 'consistency' (which appears earlier in the same sentence). The string ' against nature.stency.

' has the canonical fingerprint of a partial-replace artefact: a left-anchor word + a stray suffix from a previously-overwritten word. The fix is to delete the suffix, leaving 'against nature.' as a clean sentence end.

other_side_of_the_argument: A defender could argue: (1) the fragment is harmless, no reader will derive an incorrect inference from it; (2) editorial polish is not the analyst's job; (3) writing an EXP for a typo is over-engineering. Counter: (1) typos in published prose silently corrode the credibility of the surrounding analytical claims, even when they don't change meaning — the Curmudgeon-c-N machinery exists precisely because small textual defects compound; (2) Mode 2b explicitly authorises the analyst to write an expansion when the singleton re-examination surfaces something that needs updating, and 'updating' includes editorial cleanup of defects observed during the re-read; (3) the EXP is small (one substring patch with a unique anchor and a dry-run-verified expected_occurrences=1), the integration cost is one decider apply + build + test, and the benefit is a clean publish of WIN-044 prose. The defender's position would be stronger if there were a queue of similar editorial bugs and writing 70 EXPs were the issue — but the rest of WIN-044 is clean and this is one anchor.
propagation_note: No propagation outside WIN-044 detail_evidence. tldr_evidence, tldr_verdict, finding, detail_verdict_text, detail_extra, detail_claim, code_analysis, claim, verdict all untouched. WIN-001/010/025/057/068 (cross-referenced WINs) untouched. Sections.json untouched.
curmudgeon_verification_recommended: After integration: confirm the substring 'stency' no longer appears in WIN-044 detail_evidence (post-patch grep should match only positions 1222 — i.e. the legitimate 'consistency' occurrence). Workflow: node -e "const w=JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('data/wins.json','utf8'));const t=w.find(x=>x.id==='044').detail_evidence;let p=0,n=0;while(true){const i=t.indexOf('stency',p);if(i===-1)break;n++;p=i+1};console.log('stency occurrences:',n)" should print 1 post-patch (was 2 pre-patch).
Confirm the Self-Contradicted verdict, version-instability lead, and Section 1.8 cross-reference are all intact post-patch (no scope-leak from the typo fix into other parts of detail_evidence).

' verified count=1 in raw data/wins.json and count=1 in WIN-044.detail_evidence. Dry-run apply: post-patch detail_evidence reduced by exactly 7 characters (length 2197 → 2190).
